phase1.explanation:
ArrayList is a resizable array implementation of the List interface:

Creation:
• ArrayList<String> list = new ArrayList<>();
• ArrayList<Integer> nums = new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList(1,2,3));

Common operations:
• add(element) — appends to end
• add(index, element) — inserts at specific position
• get(index) — returns element at index
• remove(index) — removes by index
• remove(Object) — removes by object
• set(index, element) — replaces at index
• size() — returns number of elements
• contains(object) — checks if exists
• indexOf(object) — returns index (or -1)
• isEmpty() — checks if empty
• clear() — removes all
• toArray() — converts to array

Iteration:
• for-each: for (String s : list) { ... }
• for loop with index: for (int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++)
• Iterator: Iterator<String> it = list.iterator();

Key features:
• Maintains insertion order
• Allows duplicates
• Allows null values
• Fast random access (O(1))
• Resizes automatically

Performance:
• get: O(1)
• add: O(1) amortized
• remove from end: O(1)
• insert/remove from beginning: O(n)

⚠️ ArrayList vs Array:
• ArrayList grows dynamically
• ArrayList has useful methods (add, remove, contains)
• ArrayList can't store primitives (uses wrappers)

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates an ArrayList<String> of fruits: "Apple", "Banana", "Orange", "Apple", "Grape", "Banana"
2. Prints the list
3. Removes all occurrences of "Banana" using removeAll() or removeIf()
4. Prints the list again
5. Prints the size
6. Checks if the list contains "Orange"
7. Gets the element at index 2
dialogueHints: Initialize with Arrays.asList() or add individually
Use removeIf(s -> s.equals("Banana")) to remove all bananas
Use get(2) to access element at index 2
phase3.openingDialogue: ArrayList — the Swiss Army knife of collections! 🔧
